data.title: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP investigates claims for Biohaven investors from March 24, 2023 to May 14, 2025. Contact for losses.
data.description: Securities Litigation Partner James Wilson from Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is urging investors who experienced losses in Biohaven to reach out to him to explore potential strategies. If you bought or obtained Biohaven securities during the period from March 24, 2023, to May 14, 2025, and are interested in seeking guidance, Wilson is available for direct consultation. Investors are encouraged to discuss their options for a potential resolution.

data.title: Saint Mary's College teams up with Scout to enhance student-athlete financial growth and streamline NIL operations.
data.description: Saint Mary's College in Moraga, California announced on August 8, 2025, a strategic collaboration with Scout to provide compliance-ready systems, financial literacy education, and secure revenue distribution to Gael student-athletes. This partnership aims to enhance the support services available to student-athletes by offering valuable resources to help them navigate the complexities of collegiate athletics. This initiative underscores the college's commitment to ensuring the well-being and success of its student-athletes by equipping them with essential skills and tools. The collaboration is set to benefit a significant number of Gael student-athletes and strengthen the overall collegiate athletic program at Saint Mary's College.

data.title: poLight ASA's 2Q results show progress thanks to strategic markets and investments, like the Strategic Investment Agreement.
data.description: In Tønsberg, Norway, poLight ASA (PLT: OSE) reported promising results for the second quarter. The company noted significant progress in key markets and market activity attributable to the Strategic Investment Agreement.

data.title: Euroseas Ltd. (NASDAQ: ESEA) to release Q2 2025 results on Aug. 13, 2025; owner of container carrier vessels.
data.description: Athens, Greece – Euroseas Ltd. (NASDAQ: ESEA), a company managing container carrier vessels and offering seaborne transportation for containerized cargoes, disclosed its plan to unveil the financial outcomes for the second quarter concluding on June 30, 2025, on August 13, 2025, before the commencement of trading in New York.

data.title: GivBux (OTC: GBUX) expands its direct-sales channel to reach consumers nationwide, increasing utilization of its Super App.
data.description: GivBux, Inc. revealed on August 8, 2025, its plans to broaden its direct-sales channel to consumers via independent sales professionals across the nation. As a publicly traded company under the ticker symbol GBUX, GivBux, Inc. functions as a Super App and charitable giving platform. This move signifies a strategic initiative towards reaching a wider audience through a network of sales representatives.

data.title: Under Armour's stock dropped due to weak guidance and tariff concerns. Q1 revenue fell 4% to $1.13 billion, missing estimates.
data.description: Under Armour, Inc. (NYSE: UAA) stock took a hit on Friday following the release of its first-quarter fiscal 2026 results. The company's revenue decreased by 4% year over year to $1.13 billion, missing the Wall Street consensus estimate of $1.154 billion. Under Armour reported a GAAP net loss of $3 million (1 cent per diluted share) and an adjusted net income of $9 million (2 cents per share), up from 1 cent per share a year ago. In terms of regional performance, North America revenue dropped by 5% to $670 million, while international revenue saw a 1% decline to $467 million. Specifically, EMEA revenue rose by 10% to $249 million, but Asia-Pacific and Latin America revenues fell by 10% and 15%, respectively. Wholesale revenue decreased by 5% to $649 million, and direct-to-consumer revenue fell by 3% to $463 million. eCommerce revenue also saw a significant decrease of 12%, accounting for 31% of the direct-to-consumer business.

data.title: WCC Biomedical will showcase WINMAP™ microarray patch technology at NACDS Total Store Expo with 2 new products.
data.description: In Taipei on August 8, 2025, WCC Biomedical, a prominent company specializing in innovative drug delivery solutions, announced its participation in the upcoming NACDS Total Store Expo (TSE). During the event, WCC Biomedical will showcase its exclusive WINMAP™ microarray patch (MAP) technology and introduce two new products to the market. The company aims to highlight the benefits and applications of the WINMAP™ technology at the exhibition.

data.title: Avangrid hosts NHCSL Summer Meeting in Boston, convening 100 Hispanic state legislators to discuss pressing issues.
data.description: Avangrid, Inc., a prominent energy company part of the Iberdrola Group, recently organized the Summer Meeting of the National Hispanic Caucus of State Legislators (NHCSL) in Boston, MA. The event brought together close to 100 Hispanic state legislators from various parts of the United States to discuss crucial national issues, such as the increasing energy demand. Avangrid played a vital role in coordinating this gathering, which aimed to facilitate strategic dialogues.

data.title: CAE market to hit $19.96B by 2030, growing at 10.2% CAGR led by Ansys, Autodesk, & Dassault. Shift to cloud-based solutions driving growth, offering scalability & accessibility for engineers. AI & ML transforming CAE processes. Major players include Ansys, Autodesk, Dassault Systèmes, Siemens, and more.
data.description: The Computer-aided Engineering (CAE) Market is anticipated to witness significant growth, increasing from USD 12.28 billion in 2025 to USD 19.96 billion by 2030, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 10.2%. This growth is primarily fueled by the shift towards cloud-based simulation due to benefits like scalability, cost-efficiency, and accessibility. Leading companies such as Ansys, Siemens, and Dassault Systèmes are expanding their cloud-enabled CAE offerings, allowing engineers to conduct simulations without heavy upfront infrastructure, benefiting remote teams and Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs). This enhancement leads to faster collaboration, reduced time-to-market, and increased flexibility in design processes. The CAE market dynamics include drivers such as advanced simulation techniques, EVs, autonomous vehicles, IoT, and 3D printing, as well as challenges like high initial setup costs and data security concerns with cloud-based CAE. AI and Machine Learning (ML) are revolutionizing CAE by enabling quicker simulation and intelligent design, including generative design through AI-driven CAE platforms. Key companies in the CAE market include Ansys, Autodesk, Dassault Systèmes, Hexagon AB, Siemens, among others. The full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com. For more details, visit https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/pdfdownloadNew.asp?id=33357366.
